channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Business Internet Security: Everything You Need to Consider
item.description: Cyber-attacks can cost companies millions of dollars in lost revenue, legal fees, and recovery efforts. A security breach can severely tarnish a company’s reputation and customer trust, making comprehensive internet security crucial for your small business.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The 9 next big things in consumer technology for 2024
item.description: If you want to get excited about consumer tech again, look at these innovations. They include breakthroughs in material design and miniaturization to make your gadgets even sleeker; new technologies to improve your health and wellness; and some clever applications of AI and data analysis.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Comprehensive Reform Bill Passes House with Significant Changes for VA
item.description: The Dole Act passes House, making significant changes to Department of Veterans Affairs operations and funding.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: A matter of time: 3 metrics that demonstrate the value of immersion cooling
item.description: Immersion cooling in the data center space gets a lot of positive attention for the formulations that enhance thermal performance, material compatibility, and durability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Microsoft Ignite 2024 — get the latest news and insights
item.description: Microsoft Ignite 2024 kicks off in Chicago and runs Nov. 19-22. If you can’t make it to Chicago, no worries. First, the physical event is sold out, according to the Ignite event page. Second, it’s a hybrid event, so you can attend Ignite virtually.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Guías para asegurar alineación entre TI y empresa
item.description: Recomendaciones clave para garantizar una estrecha coordinación entre tecnología e industria empresarial.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump expected to attend SpaceX launch on Tuesday
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to attend the launch of a SpaceX rocket in Texas on Tuesday, sources familiar with the plans confirmed, the latest sign of Trump’s closeness to the company's billionaire CEO Elon Musk. The launch is scheduled for 5 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft rebrands Azure AI Studio to Azure AI Foundry
item.description: Microsoft is packaging its Azure AI Studio and other updates into a new service — Azure AI Foundry in response to enterprises’ need to develop, run, and manage generative AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 4 monkeys remain free nearly 2 weeks after dozens escaped a South Carolina compound
item.description: Authorities say four monkeys remain free nearly two weeks after a group of 43 escaped from a South Carolina compound that breeds them for medical research.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 11 ways to ensure IT-business alignment
item.description: Most IT and business executives recognize the necessity of close alignment. They understand that their strategies, capabilities, resources, and management systems should be configured to support the enterprise’s overarching purpose and goals.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — A federal judge in Delaware on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How a wrong Google search can compromise your data and bring law enforcement calling
item.description: Wrong Google searches can not only compromise your device and personal data. They can also bring law enforcement to your doorstep. Hackers are targeting Google searches.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU fines Meta over $840 million for ‘abusive practices’ favoring Facebook Marketplace
item.description: Meta received a fine of over $840 million on Wednesday for breaching the European Union’s antitrust rules as it relates to market domination after three years of legal proceedings.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google’s Gemini app is now available on iPhones
item.description: Google has entered a new and more intense phase of the AI wars, introducing its own Google Gemini app for iPhones; now you can use Apple Intelligence, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot and Google Gemini on one device.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work now focuses on the planned successor release, JDK 24, which has ballooned to 21 proposed features.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Rust Foundation moves forward on C++ and Rust interoperability
item.description: The Rust Foundation, which stewards development of the Rust language, has released a statement addressing challenges and opportunities for interoperability between Rust and C++, with the intent of making cross-language development more accessible and approachable.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
